steps.title: Identify the slope
steps.content: The equation $g(x+1)-g(x)=3$ means the output increases by $3$ whenever the input increases by $1$. Since $g$ is linear, its slope is $3$.

steps.title: Find the constant term
steps.content:
Write the function as $g(x)=3x+b$. Use $g(8)=13$ to solve for $b$.
$$13=3(8)+b$$
$$13=24+b$$
$$b=-11$$

steps.title: Write the function
steps.content:
Substitute the constant term back into the linear equation.
$$g(x)=3x-11$$
The answer is D.
